sections.content:
The code executed 1 action in the previous sequence.
Executed actions: MOUSE(row=21, col=42).
You are still on the same level.
Current state: step 26, level 1.
Valid actions right now: MOUSE.
Only tool: `python`. It receives `current_frame`, `previous_frame`, `history`, `transitions`, `last_transition`, `valid_actions`, `last_action_result`, and `action(actions)`. It also provides `last_animation` (per-action frames of your last `action(...)` call) and `frame_stats` (how much this game has animated so far) -- optional; use them only if you judge the intermediate motion informative rather than incidental.
Only letter-coded board views and lightweight metadata are exposed; raw numeric color IDs are not available.
Keep tool output compact: use `current_frame.segmentation` as the primary view, and `current_frame.ascii` only for a small specific region; never print full boards.
For the most recent change, compare `previous_frame` to `current_frame`, or `last_transition.before_frame` to `last_transition.after_frame`; `history[-1].frame` is the current frame, not the previous one.
Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.
Maintain a compact working world model of what the current level seems to contain, what actions appear to do, what the goal seems to be, what is still uncertain, and what plan currently looks best.
Below you are provided with the current world model from the previous turn. The default behavior is to copy it and add or remove things based on the evidence that you gathered. BEFORE EXECUTING NEW ACTIONS YOU MUST ALWAYS GIVE THE REVISED VERSION OF THE WORLD MODEL.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it, but stop immediately if a result reports `game_over`, `level_completed`, or `done`.
Working world model carried from earlier turns:
- World model: The board has green and yellow bars that can be shrunk/expanded by clicking on U-shaped controls. I've explored all 4 bar configurations without completing the level. I haven't tried clicking on the G strips (gray objects between/around the U-shapes).
- Goal model: Unclear. The bars toggle but don't complete the level. Need to find what action actually solves the level.
- Plan: Try clicking on the G strip between the green U-shapes (node 11, at col 42, row 21) to see if it does something.
- Revise any item above immediately if `current_frame` or `history` contradicts it.
end of world model. 
Focus on what changed most recently in `history`, update the target environment change if needed, and separate gameplay-object changes from HUD-only changes.
When ready, call `action(actions)` from inside the `python` tool with the best valid action or ordered batch selected by your code. If your code has found a reliable short sequence, prefer batching it in one call.
You may call `action(actions)` more than once in one Python snippet if your search or control loop needs it.
If you include assistant text before a tool call, keep it short and use it to update the world model. Helpful optional prefixes are `World model:`, `Goal model:`, `Action model:`, `Recent findings:`, `Open questions:`, `Plan:`, and `Cross-level notes:`.
When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. Use only that format; do not add markdown fences, prose wrappers, or alternate tool-call syntax. Do not quote or place tool-call markup inside explanatory text; when you decide to call the tool, emit the tool call itself.
If you use MOUSE, include integer row and col arguments.
EFFICIENCY BUDGET — your score on each level is (baseline_actions / your_actions)^2, so every wasted action costs you quadratically.
Level 1: you have used 25 actions; a strong score needs about 20 or fewer. You are 1.2x over the target.
If you are not steadily making progress toward the level goal, commit to your single best hypothesis and the shortest sequence that tests it — do not exhaustively scan rows/columns or enumerate every option. Test one idea, read the result, then decide.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking on the G strip (row 21, col 42) was no_impact. So the G strips don't do anything either.
Let me think about this more carefully. I've tried:
1. Clicking on the green U-shapes - toggles green bar
2. Clicking on the yellow U-shapes - toggles yellow bar
3. Clicking on the red cross patterns - no_impact
4. Clicking on the green bar itself - no_impact
5. Clicking on the yellow bar itself - no_impact
6. Clicking on the black background - no_impact
7. Clicking on the G strips - no_impact
I've visited all 4 bar configurations and none completed the level. So the goal is not just to reach a specific bar configuration.
Wait, let me reconsider. Maybe the goal is to click on the red cross patterns, but only when the bars are in a specific state. I tried clicking on the red cross patterns from various states, but they were all no_impact.
Actually, let me check which states I clicked the red cross patterns from. Looking at the transitions:
- Transition 0: MOUSE(row=10, col=52) - this is the top red cross pattern, from state N=28, Y=37 (both full)
- Transition 6: MOUSE(row=9, col=52) - this is the top red cross pattern, from state N=19, Y=37 (green shrunk, yellow full)
- Transition 12: MOUSE(row=18, col=42) - this is NOT a red cross pattern
- Transition 13: MOUSE(row=52, col=10) - this is the bottom red cross pattern, from state N=19, Y=28 (both shrunk)
- Transition 18: MOUSE(row=10, col=52) - this is the top red cross pattern, from state N=19, Y=28 (both shrunk)
- Transition 23: MOUSE(row=52, col=10) - this is the bottom red cross pattern, from state N=19, Y=28 (both shrunk)
So I've clicked on the red cross patterns from states:
- N=28, Y=37 (both full)
- N=19, Y=37 (green shrunk, yellow full)
- N=19, Y=28 (both shrunk)
I haven't clicked on the red cross patterns from state N=28, Y=28 (green full, yellow shrunk). Let me try that.
But first, I need to get to that state. The current state is N=19, Y=28 (both shrunk). To get to N=28, Y=28, I need to expand the green bar by clicking on (20, 45).
Wait, but I've already tried clicking on the red cross patterns from multiple states and they were all no_impact. It seems unlikely that clicking on them from a different state would work.
Let me think about this differently. What if the goal is to click on the center of the U-shapes (the empty space inside)? For example, the left green U-shape has N pixels at [20, 38], [20, 39], [20, 40], [21, 38], [22, 38], [22, 39], [22, 40]. The center of this U-shape would be at [21, 39] or [21, 40].
Actually, looking at the U-shape, it's a "C" shape (open on the right side). The center would be at [21, 39] or [21, 40], which is inside the U-shape but not on the N pixels.
Let me try clicking on the center of the left green U-shape, which is at [21, 39].
Wait, but I already clicked on (20, 39) which is on the N pixel of the left green U-shape. And (21, 39) is inside the U-shape but not on the N pixel.
Actually, looking at the ascii for row 21:
`BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBgcNcccGcccNcgBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB`
The left green U-shape has N at col 38, and the right green U-shape has N at col 46. The center between them is cols 39-45.
Wait, let me re-examine. The left green U-shape is at cols 38-40 (N at [20, 38], [20, 39], [20, 40], [21, 38], [22, 38], [22, 39], [22, 40]). This is a "C" shape open on the right side. The center of the U-shape would be at [21, 39] and [21, 40], which are inside the U-shape.
Looking at row 21, col 39 is 'c' (charcoal) and col 40 is 'c'. So the center of the U-shape is charcoal, not N.
Let me try clicking on [21, 39], which is inside the left green U-shape.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking on G strips is no_impact. I've tried clicking on U-shapes (toggles bars), red crosses (no_impact), bars themselves (no_impact), background (no_impact), and G strips (no_impact). All 4 bar configurations visited without level completion.
Goal model: Still unclear. Maybe need to click inside the U-shapes (the empty center), or click on red crosses from a specific bar state I haven't tried (N=28, Y=28).
Plan: Try clicking inside the left green U-shape at [21, 39] (the empty center of the C-shape).

<function=python>
<parameter=code>

# The left green U-shape is at cols 38-40, rows 20-22
# The center would be at [21, 39] or [21, 40]
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 21, 'col': 39}])
print("Result:", last_action_result)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
